rulings.Case Title: EUGENE MILLARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSSDAY, APRIL 26, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Robertshaw Controls Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (sophisticated user defense) and off calendar as to issues 2 and 3 (strict liability, false representation and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense is applicable regarding asbestos-containing fireproofing. Plaintiff has dismissed his strict liability and false representation causes of action and his claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LIMA VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Impose Sanctions Against Keller Fishback Jackson Under Ccp Section 128.7(B)(1) (2)
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSSDAY, APRIL 26, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Berry & Berry¿s motion to impose sanctions of $5,240 against Keller Fishback & Jackson is denied. Berry & Berry has not demonstrated, pursuant to C.C.P. 128.7(b), that plaintiff¿s previous disqualification motion was brought for an improper or harassing purpose or that plaintiff¿s arguments were frivolous. Although Berry & Berry¿s memorandum of points and authorities mentions a request for additional sanctions payable to the court, such request was not properly included in the notice of motion, and thus is denied. (C.C.P. 2023.040.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
